label: Recorded crime incidents
note:
Data may be subject to revision. Based on data extracted 01 March 2026.
.. [b]Fraud, Deception & Related offences[/b] from Q3 2023 onwards has been revised due to the effect of the backlog in processing suspected fraud crime incidents reported to the Gardaí by Financial Institutions under Section 19 of the Criminal Justice Act, 2011. It is no longer comparable to those published for the period prior to Q3 2023. Please see [url=https://www.cso.ie/en/releasesandpublications/in/rc/march2025informationnoteonfraudcrimestatistics/]information note[/url] for further details. For revised figures from Q3 2023 please see PxStat tables [url=table/CJQ07]CJQ07[/url] & [url=table/CJQ08]CJQ08[/url].
The ’[b]Under Reservation[/b]’ designation has been removed from Recorded Crime Statistics.
For further information please see our [url=https://www.cso.ie/en/methods/crime/liftingofunderreservationcategorisationforrecordedcrimestatisticsfaq/]Lifting of Under Reservation categorisation for Recorded Crime Statistics FAQ page[/url] on our website.
From 28 January 2024, policing services have been transferred from the Laytown sub-district in the Meath Division to the Drogheda sub-district in the Louth Division. This means also that from this date policing services that were in the Laytown sub-district have moved from the Eastern to the North Western Garda region.
For more information, please go to the [url=https://www.cso.ie/en/statistics/crimeandjustice/recordedcrime/]statistical release page[/url] on our website.
